verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 (TGF-β1) is a secreted cytokine. It is synthesized as a preproprotein, processed in the ER/Golgi, and secreted into the extracellular space. The mature active form circulates in the bloodstream and interstitial fluid. It does not possess a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any intrinsic mechanism to remain stably anchored to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. While TGFB1 binds to cell-surface serine/threonine kinase receptors (TGFBR1/ALK5 and TGFBR2), this is a transient, reversible ligand–receptor interaction; the protein itself is not a membrane component. Upon wash-out, TGFB1 leaves with the soluble pool. The protein's only surface 'association' is equilibrium binding to a receptor, which does not satisfy the recruitment test (own mechanism to remain anchored). Therefore, it is excluded as a surface-accessible target.
